Enzymatic glycine removal leaving an amidated C-terminus

project.title: Exosomes of p53 mutant H1299 cells
project.projectDescription: Proteome of exosomes purified from H1299-p53R273H (mutant) and H1299-p53-/- cells, which found the levels of Podocalyxin altered in p53-mutant cells.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: H1299-p53-/- cells were cultured in heavy SILAC (lysine8, arginine10 – Cambridge Isotope Labs) and H1299-p53R273H in light SILAC medium. Conditioned media from these labelled cells were mixed and exosomes isolated by differential centrifugation. The exosome pellet was re-suspended in 6M urea for mass spectrometry analysis. Exosome proteins were reduced (10 mM dithiothreitol), alkylated (55 mM iodoacetamide) and digested (Lys C and trypsin). Peptides were cleaned using stage tips and re-dissolved in 5 % acetonitrile/0.25 % formic acid. Protein samples were then applied directly to an Orbitrap Elite (LC-MS).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Data was searched and quantified using Swissprot (Human) database and MaxQuant software (version 1.5.0.36).

references.referenceLine: Novo D, Heath N, Mitchell L, Caligiuri G, MacFarlane A, Reijmer D, Charlton L, Knight J, Calka M, McGhee E, Dornier E, Sumpton D, Mason S, Echard A, Klinkert K, Secklehner J, Kruiswijk F, Vousden K, Macpherson IR, Blyth K, Bailey P, Yin H, Carlin LM, Morton J, Zanivan S, Norman JC. Mutant p53s generate pro-invasive niches by influencing exosome podocalyxin levels. Nat Commun. 2018 9(1):5069

identifiedPTMStrings.name: No PTMs are included in the dataset

Comment[Instrument]: FEI Tecnai T20 running at 200 kV
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: Exosomes were then pelleted using a 100,000g centrifugation in a SW32 rotor.
Comment[NumberOfFractions]: Exosomes were overlaid with 11 layers of sucrose deceasing in concentration (from 2 to 0.4 M sucrose using 20 nm HEPES as the diluent).
